page: OVHcloud r2-120 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) running at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 120.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 200 GB SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. One complimentary public IPv4 address is included.

Benchmark results indicate a clear tradeoff between compute power and memory capacity. The instance exhibits poor single-core performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10%. Multi-core CPU performance is also weak, placing in the bottom 25%. However, memory subsystem performance is more balanced; the Passmark memory score and small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth of 582.18 GB/sec both rank in the middle 50%. Conversely, large-block uncached write bandwidth is poor, landing in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r2-120 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-heavy workloads that do not require intense CPU calculations. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform at average levels, with Redis achieving 8.58 million operations per second. This profile is suitable for caching layers, in-memory databases, and moderate database operations, but it is less suited for CPU-bound tasks like large-scale LLM inference or heavy text processing.
description: OVHcloud r2-120 is a memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated physical Intel cores running at 2.2 GHz, paired with 120.0 GB of RAM and a 200 GB local SSD. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per core. Benchmark data reveals a significant tradeoff: while single-core and multi-core CPU performance ranks in the bottom 10% to 25%, memory-centric operations such as small-block cached reads and Redis SET operations achieve average tier results. With a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps and one included public IPv4 address, this instance is structured for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high compute performance. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and moderate database operations.
og_description: OVHcloud r2-120 is a memory-optimized KVM server with 8 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, offering high memory density for database and caching workloads despite lower CPU performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud r2-120 is a memory-optimized KVM server featuring 8 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-core ratio and local SSD storage for memory-intensive caching and database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated physical Intel cores at 2.2 GHz
120 GB of system memory providing 15 GB per core
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Average performance in Redis and database operations
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy workloads
